title: Police seek Mass. vehicle after driver involved in fatal Providence hit-and-run
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Providence Police seek a black Chevrolet Equinox involved in a hit-and-run, registered in Massachusetts.
At 2:35 a.m. Saturday, a pedestrian, Kevin Chappie, was fatally struck near Corliss Street in Providence.
Police urge public assistance in locating the driver, asking for tips via 401-243-6279 or 401-243-6285.

title: Martha Stewart’s The Bedford is now open at this New England resort
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Bedford by Martha Stewart opened at Foxwoods Resort Casino in Connecticut on March 14.
The 10,000-square-foot restaurant seats 204 guests and serves Martha Stewart's favorite recipes.
The menu features exclusive dishes for Foxwoods, with breakfast and dinner daily, plus lunch Friday-Sunday.

title: Weekend commuter rail disruption will now leak into Fridays
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA and Keolis will extend commuter rail shutdowns at North Station to include several Fridays for signal upgrades.
Train service disruptions are scheduled on weekends of March 20-22, March 27-29, April 10-12, and April 24-27.
Shuttle buses replace trains on Lowell, Newburyport/Rockport, Haverhill, and Fitchburg Lines during the scheduled outages.

title: This Massachusetts commute ranks as the most stressful, poll finds
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A poll by Munley Law ranks Zakim Bridge/I-93 North in Boston as Massachusetts' most stressful commute.
The Southeast Expressway and I-95/Route 128 in Waltham are also high on the list of stressful Massachusetts commutes.
Boston's traffic issues are comparable to major cities like New York and Los Angeles, highlighting regional infrastructure challenges.

title: Jaylen Brown describes ‘surreal’ experience at his first St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown attended his first St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, describing the event as surreal and enjoyable.
Brown streamed his parade experience on Twitch, engaging with fans by catching a football, dancing, and waving.
Brown, a Boston Celtic, is having a career year, averaging 28.4 points, 7.1 rebounds, and 5.1 assists per game.

title: Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A resident brought a suspected mortar shell into the Essex Police Department, leading to an evacuation.
The Massachusetts State Police Bomb Squad determined the item was a non-explosive, empty fuel canister.
Police advise against transporting suspicious items, urging the public to call 911 instead.

title: Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest Airlines' three-day sale offers one-way flights starting at $59, valid for travel April 7 to June 10.
Discounted fares apply to Tuesday and Wednesday flights, within the continental U.S. and inter-island Hawaii travel.
Flights from Boston include $59 to Baltimore, $94 to Cleveland, $109 to Savannah, and $160 to San Diego.

title: City councilors call on Boston police to release footage of fatal shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police shot and killed Stephenson King during a carjacking incident in Roxbury on March 11, 2026.
City councilors Miniard Culpepper and Brian Worrell urge police to release body camera footage for transparency.
Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox confirmed body camera footage exists and Suffolk DA's investigation is ongoing.

title: JPMorganChase inks deal to become the main tenant of new South Station Tower
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: JPMorganChase will move its Boston offices to South Station Tower in 2028 as the anchor tenant, occupying 250,000 square feet.
The relocation will bring approximately 1,000 employees to the new location, with plans to add 300 jobs in coming years.
JPMorganChase opened its first Massachusetts branch in 2018 and plans to open its 100th branch later in 2026.

title: Here’s the versatile wide receiver Mel Kiper has the Patriots picking 31st overall in his latest mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Mel Kiper Jr.'s latest mock draft projects Patriots to select K.C. Concepcion, a wide receiver from Texas A&M, at 31st.
Concepcion recorded 61 catches for 919 yards and nine touchdowns in 2025, with two return touchdowns.
Kiper believes Concepcion's versatility will benefit Patriots' quarterback Drake Maye by stretching the field.
